question: How many funds currently have a Critical effective risk tier?
reference_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT rs.scope_id) AS critical_fund_count FROM risk_scores rs WHERE rs.scope = 'fund' AND COALESCE(rs.escalated_tier, rs.direct_tier) = 'critical'
notes: Tests COALESCE(escalated_tier, direct_tier) business logic. F1 (Northgate Capital Partners LP) has direct_tier=low but escalated_tier=critical, making effective tier critical.

question: Which fund has the highest direct risk score, and what is that score?
reference_sql: SELECT f.name AS fund_name, rs.direct_score FROM funds f JOIN risk_scores rs ON rs.scope_id = f.fund_id WHERE rs.scope = 'fund' ORDER BY rs.direct_score DESC LIMIT 1
notes: F4 (Harrington Private Capital) direct_score=51.5 (High tier). F1 has higher effective tier (Critical) but lower direct_score (11.0) due to BLE escalation.

question: Which funds have at least one document with status expired?
reference_sql: SELECT DISTINCT f.name AS fund_name FROM funds f JOIN fund_documents fd ON fd.fund_id = f.fund_id WHERE fd.status = 'expired' ORDER BY f.name
notes: Only F2 (Meridian Strategic Growth Trust) has an expired document: Annual Report, expiry 2026-05-06, status=expired.

question: Which BLE has a confirmed sanctions screening hit, and which fund does it belong to?
reference_sql: SELECT f.name AS fund_name, cp.institution_name AS counterparty_name, b.location AS ble_location, sr.hit_type FROM screening_results sr JOIN counterparty_profiles cp ON cp.counterparty_id = sr.scope_id JOIN bles b ON b.counterparty_profile_id = cp.counterparty_id JOIN funds f ON f.fund_id = b.parent_fund_id WHERE sr.scope = 'counterparty' AND sr.result_status = 'hit' AND sr.hit_severity = 'confirmed'
notes: Tests counterparty-scope screening lane (scope='counterparty'). Bank Rossiya is a real sanctioned entity; the hit is a real OpenSanctions result expected in non-mock mode.

question: How many distinct counterparty institutions are referenced across all BLEs?
reference_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT counterparty_profile_id) AS distinct_counterparties FROM bles
notes: DBS Bank Ltd is shared across two BLEs (F2/Singapore and F3/Hong Kong) but counts once. Total: Bank Rossiya, Deutsche Bank AG, DBS Bank Ltd, Emirates NBD Bank PJSC, ICBC Limited, Standard Chartered Bank.

question: List every Fund, BLE, and product combination: show the fund name, counterparty name, BLE location, and product type for all active relationships.
reference_sql: SELECT f.name AS fund_name, cp.institution_name AS counterparty_name, b.location AS ble_location, bp.product_type FROM funds f JOIN bles b ON b.parent_fund_id = f.fund_id JOIN counterparty_profiles cp ON cp.counterparty_id = b.counterparty_profile_id JOIN ble_products bp ON bp.ble_id = b.ble_id ORDER BY f.name, cp.institution_name, bp.product_type
notes: Required Fund->BLE->Product three-table join. 8 rows: F5 (Queensbridge) has 3 rows because ICBC carries both Loan and Cash products under one BLE. Eval comparison is set-based (order-independent).

question: How many UBO records across all funds carry a PEP tier of 1 or higher?
reference_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS pep_ubo_count FROM ubo_records WHERE pep_tier >= 1
notes: Two UBOs: Robert Harrington III (PEP tier 1, F4) and Werner Mueller (PEP tier 2, F2). BLE PEP context (Ahmad Al-Rashidi) is NOT a ubo_records entry and must not be counted here.

question: Which fund was automatically escalated to a higher effective risk tier than its direct tier, and what was the escalation reason?

expected_result.escalation_reason: Escalated to Critical due to BLE(s): Bank Rossiya (Moscow, Russia)

reference_sql: SELECT f.name AS fund_name, rs.direct_tier, rs.escalated_tier, rs.escalation_reason FROM risk_scores rs JOIN funds f ON f.fund_id = rs.scope_id WHERE rs.scope = 'fund' AND rs.escalated_tier IS NOT NULL ORDER BY f.name
notes: Tests PRD §9.3 escalation rule. Only F1 has escalated_tier set (non-null). The escalation_reason text must match exactly as stored; generated SQL should return it verbatim.

question: How many Loan products are active across all funds?
reference_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS loan_product_count FROM ble_products WHERE product_type = 'Loan'
notes: Four Loan products: F1/Bank Rossiya, F2/DBS Singapore, F3/DBS Hong Kong, F5/ICBC Mumbai. F2/Deutsche Bank carries Cash only; F4/Emirates NBD carries Cash only; F5/Standard Chartered carries Cash only.

question: Which funds have more than one BLE, and how many BLEs does each have?
reference_sql: SELECT f.name AS fund_name, COUNT(b.ble_id) AS ble_count FROM funds f JOIN bles b ON b.parent_fund_id = f.fund_id GROUP BY f.fund_id, f.name HAVING COUNT(b.ble_id) > 1 ORDER BY f.name
notes: F2 (Meridian) has Deutsche Bank AG (Frankfurt) and DBS Bank Ltd (Singapore). F5 (Queensbridge) has ICBC Limited (Mumbai) and Standard Chartered Bank (Singapore). All other funds have exactly one BLE.
